ai_strategy_lead.name: Kevin Yee
ai_strategy_lead.title: Special Assistant to the Provost for Artificial Intelligence; Director, Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning

documents.title: UCF Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI) announcement
UCF Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI) landing page

ai_strategy.summary: UCF's AI strategy is anchored by two complementary bodies: the Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI) launched July 30, 2025 under Trustee Chair Mubarak Shah, and the AI for All initiative led by Kevin Yee as Special Assistant to the Provost for AI. The IAI unites 25 faculty across the Colleges of Business, Engineering and Computer Science, Medicine, and Sciences around foundational and applied AI (computer vision, robotics, ML, healthcare, finance, mathematics) and is explicitly positioned to attract industry partners such as Amazon, Google and Meta to Central Florida. AI for All is a parallel teaching-and-learning strategy to scale AI fluency across every discipline through courses, the annual Teaching and Learning with AI Conference, and an academic governance council.

key_provisions: Intentional AI-tool use must be disclosed (tool name, date); embedded assistants like autocorrect/spell-check exempt
Prohibits uploading copyrighted/proprietary materials (including student work and UCF library articles) to AI tools that train or store data
Faculty discouraged from relying on AI detection tools due to unreliability and false positives/negatives
Sensitive UCF business or proprietary information must not be shared with AI tools
Microsoft Copilot with Commercial Data Protection is the approved enterprise tool for sensitive data
Students must not misrepresent AI output as original work; should view AI as a thought partner
Staff must de-identify sensitive data before any AI exposure
ai_policy.summary: UCF has not enacted a stand-alone AI Policy but operates under a comprehensive set of ethical guidelines co-authored by UCF IT, Libraries, Center for Ethics, Center for Distributed Learning, FCTL, Faculty Senate IT Committee, and the Office of Research. The stance is comprehensive: disclosure is required, copyright and privacy are protected, enterprise Copilot is the sanctioned tool for sensitive data, AI detectors are disfavored, and syllabus-level discretion is left to faculty.

bodies.name: Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI)
bodies.scope: University-wide research institute coordinating AI faculty, education and industry engagement across four colleges

bodies.name: AI for All Initiative
bodies.scope: University-wide teaching, learning and AI fluency initiative led by the Special Assistant to the Provost for AI

bodies.name: Academic Governance Council for AI
bodies.scope: Faculty governance body coordinating AI curriculum, policy input, and discipline-level liaisons within the AI for All initiative

bodies.name: Faculty Senate IT Committee (AI guidance partner)
bodies.scope: Faculty committee participating in authorship of Responsible AI Use guidelines

bodies.name: Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning (FCTL) — AI program
bodies.scope: Provides AI syllabus statements, faculty training and academic integrity guidance

governance_structure.summary: UCF's AI governance is a hub-and-spokes structure. The Institute of Artificial Intelligence owns research and talent; AI for All (reporting to the provost via Kevin Yee) owns teaching, learning and fluency, supported by an Academic Governance Council. Responsible-use guidelines are co-authored by UCF IT, Libraries, Center for Ethics, FCTL, Faculty Senate IT Committee, CDL and the Office of Research.

initiatives.title: Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI) launched
initiatives.description: UCF launched a university-wide Institute of Artificial Intelligence on July 30, 2025, naming Trustee Chair Mubarak Shah as inaugural director. The IAI unites an initial 25 faculty across the Colleges of Business, Engineering and Computer Science, Medicine, and Sciences, and absorbs the Center for Research in Computer Vision. Focus areas span computer vision, robotics, ML, healthcare AI, fintech and mathematics, and the institute is explicitly positioned to attract firms like Amazon, Google and Meta to Central Florida.
initiatives.significance: Establishes a nationally visible, cross-college AI enterprise at one of the largest R1 universities in the country and formalizes UCF's pivot from a collection of AI labs to a coordinated AI institution.

investment.funding_source: UCF strategic investment (Provost, VP Research); external awards from DARPA, IARPA, NSF and industry partners
investment.description: Multi-million-dollar strategic investment reflected in 11 new faculty hired in prior two years, 8 more joining fall 2025, and dedicated A.I. Initiative funding for H100 GPU infrastructure.

collaborations.description: Founding industry, health and defense partners named at IAI launch.

initiatives.policy_implications: Centralizes AI research governance and standardizes industry engagement across four colleges.

initiatives.title: AI for All initiative — AI fluency across every major
initiatives.description: AI for All, led by Special Assistant to the Provost Kevin Yee, coordinates AI curriculum, tools, ethics and fluency for students, faculty and staff. It includes the Academic Governance Council, departmental liaisons, and a dual 'lean in / introduce AI friction' pedagogical philosophy. Two new special topics courses open to all majors debut in Spring 2026: CAP 1931 (AI for All) and PAD 3930 (AI for the Workplace).
initiatives.significance: Operationalizes AI literacy as a graduation-relevant skill at one of the largest U.S. public universities, with an explicit no-prerequisite pathway for non-CS majors.

investment.description: Dedicated provost-level staffing, curriculum development across colleges, and an annual international conference.

partners: Higher education conference community (Teaching and Learning with AI Conference)
collaborations.description: Annual Teaching and Learning with AI Conference for faculty, staff and administrators internationally.

initiatives.policy_implications: Frames AI as mandatory workforce literacy while deferring syllabus-level use rules to individual faculty.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot with Commercial Data Protection — campus-wide deployment
initiatives.description: UCF has licensed Microsoft Copilot (Copilot Chat / Copilot Web) with Enterprise/Commercial Data Protection for all students, faculty and staff. Data entered is not stored or used for training, making it the university's approved tool for processing sensitive information. The deployment is framed as a 'walled garden' and is promoted through UCF IT, CDL and FCTL.
initiatives.significance: Campus-wide sanctioned generative AI tool covering 70,000+ users with data-protection guarantees sets UCF apart from peers that restrict generative AI to pilots.

investment.description: Enterprise license to Copilot covering employees and students.

collaborations.description: Enterprise vendor agreement for Copilot with Commercial Data Protection.

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot (Chat/Web) with Commercial Data Protection
tools_and_vendors.role: Sanctioned generative AI assistant for sensitive data and daily use

initiatives.policy_implications: Pairs enterprise tooling with disclosure and data-protection rules, consolidating AI usage inside a governed environment.

initiatives.title: Knightbot — AI-powered student support chatbot
initiatives.description: Knightbot, launched January 2023 and built on Mainstay's platform with a curated (non-generative) knowledge base, now serves 70,000+ students across 50+ campus units. It has handled 459,000+ messages from 277,000 unique users, resolves 85% of queries without human intervention, and freed ~12,000 staff hours. Targeted retention campaigns reduced drop rates by 15% for at-risk students.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates measurable retention and service-efficiency impact from a curated (non-generative) chatbot at very large R1 scale, and supplies a reference design for other public universities wary of hallucinations.

investment.description: Enterprise chatbot license and in-house knowledge curation across 50+ units.

tools_and_vendors.name: Knightbot
tools_and_vendors.role: Curated-knowledge AI chatbot for academics, financial aid, wellbeing

initiatives.policy_implications: Bounded, non-generative design reduces hallucination and FERPA exposure relative to open LLM deployments.

initiatives.title: Barry S. Miller College of Business — $50M gift establishing AI, fintech and disinformation chairs
initiatives.description: UCF announced a $50 million gift from alumnus Barry Miller '95 to establish the Barry S. Miller College of Business. The gift funds five endowed faculty chairs in fintech, AI strategy, cyber risk, trust and disinformation; a new master's in technology leadership and innovation; and expanded applied-learning opportunities. It is the largest single philanthropic gift in UCF's history.
initiatives.significance: Named endowed chairs in AI strategy and trust/disinformation signal permanent faculty capacity on AI policy and enterprise adoption, not a one-off program.

investment.funding_source: Barry Miller (alumnus, Voloridge Investment Management / Voloridge Health)
investment.description: Largest single gift in UCF history; funds 5 endowed chairs (fintech, AI strategy, cyber risk, trust and disinformation, one additional), a new MS in technology leadership and innovation, and applied-learning expansion.

collaborations.description: Alumnus-donor tied to quantitative investing and healthcare firms.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates permanent faculty lines on AI governance, cyber risk and disinformation — areas usually staffed on soft money.

initiatives.title: Newton GPU cluster H100 expansion funded by UCF AI Initiative
initiatives.description: UCF's Newton GPU cluster (managed by the Advanced Research Computing Center) now includes 42 NVIDIA V100 GPUs (DURIP/UCF), 58 NVIDIA H100 GPUs (EDA funding) and an additional 32 NVIDIA H100 GPUs across 4 nodes with NDR InfiniBand funded specifically by the UCF A.I. Initiative. The broader ARCC facility includes the 8,800+ core Stokes CPU cluster and 660 TB of storage.
initiatives.significance: Dedicated H100 capacity funded by the institutional AI initiative places UCF in the top tier of public-university AI compute and backs the IAI research agenda.

investment.description: Multi-source funding with a dedicated AI Initiative tranche for 32 H100 GPUs with NDR InfiniBand.

collaborations.description: Federal capital grants complement institutional funding.

initiatives.title: DARPA TIAMAT grant for autonomous systems training ($1.2M)
initiatives.description: UCF researchers George Atia and Yue Wang received a $1.23M DARPA award through the Transfer Learning from Imprecise and Abstract Models to Autonomous Technologies (TIAMAT) program to improve how robots and autonomous systems are trained.
initiatives.significance: Adds to a DARPA/IARPA/NSF funding pipeline that distinguishes UCF as a defense-AI research destination in the Southeast.

investment.description: Two-faculty award to improve autonomous systems training via transfer learning.

initiatives.title: $7.6M U.S. Department of Education study on AI-enhanced classroom chatbots
initiatives.description: UCF partners with the National Institute for Student Success at Georgia State University, Perimeter College at GSU and Morgan State University on a $7.6M U.S. Department of Education grant to test AI-enhanced chatbots in foundational math and English courses, with piloting beginning fall 2024. Knightbot is one of the chatbots being evaluated.
initiatives.significance: Cross-institution study tying UCF's Knightbot deployment to randomized evidence on retention and equity impacts — rare at this scale.

investment.funding_source: U.S. Department of Education (awarded to NISS at Georgia State; UCF is a partner site)
investment.description: Multi-site study of AI chatbots in introductory gateway courses with focus on first-generation and lower-income students.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI Use guidelines (multi-stakeholder)
initiatives.description: UCF published ethical guidelines for responsible AI use authored jointly by UCF IT, UCF Libraries, UCF Center for Ethics, Center for Distributed Learning, Faculty Center for Teaching & Learning, Faculty Senate IT Committee, the Office of Research, and departmental/college AI coordinators. The guidelines cover disclosure, intellectual property, AI detection avoidance, data protection, and faculty discretion.
initiatives.significance: A co-authored ethics-first framework (rather than a single-office policy) is a model for how a large R1 can produce comprehensive, cross-functional AI guidance without a single chief AI officer.

investment.description: Cross-office drafting and publication of ethical guidelines.

initiatives.policy_implications: Frames UCF's default posture as comprehensive and disclosure-based while leaving classroom discretion to faculty.

initiatives.title: AI, Big Data, and Human Impacts undergraduate certificate
initiatives.description: A 12-hour interdisciplinary undergraduate certificate housed in the Department of Writing and Rhetoric (College of Arts and Humanities). Students examine the ethics, histories, and societal implications of AI and big-data technologies. A complementary Digital Humanities in the Age of AI graduate certificate is offered.
initiatives.significance: Puts humanities faculty at the center of AI ethics instruction, counterbalancing the STEM-led IAI.

investment.description: Academic certificate — no dedicated external funding reported.

initiatives.title: MS in Computer Vision
initiatives.description: A specialized Master of Science in Computer Vision, offered through the Center for Research in Computer Vision (now part of IAI). Curriculum covers image/video acquisition and analysis, 3D data, and modern machine-learning techniques.
initiatives.significance: One of the nation's few named Computer Vision master's degrees; UCF's computer vision program is ranked #8 nationally.

investment.funding_source: UCF College of Engineering and Computer Science
investment.description: Specialized MS program tied to CRCV research enterprise.

collaborations.description: Pipeline into DoD and industry CV roles.

initiatives.title: MS in Data Analytics — AI Track
initiatives.description: Interdisciplinary 30-credit-hour MS in Data Analytics jointly offered by Computer Science and Statistics, with an explicit Artificial Intelligence track emphasizing technical AI methods in analytics.
initiatives.significance: Provides a non-thesis, industry-oriented AI credential at graduate level, complementing the research-heavy MS in Computer Vision.

investment.funding_source: UCF College of Engineering and Computer Science / College of Sciences

initiatives.title: Annual Teaching and Learning with AI Conference (4th annual, 2026)
initiatives.description: UCF hosts the Teaching and Learning with AI Conference, a peer-sharing event for higher-education faculty, staff, librarians and administrators. The fourth annual edition runs June 11-13, 2026 at Gaylord Palms in Orlando with no virtual option.
initiatives.significance: UCF has become a national convening hub on AI in teaching — a soft-power position that amplifies its AI for All framework.

investment.funding_source: UCF AI for All / CDL / FCTL

initiatives.title: AI Mentorship and Scholarship Initiative for students
initiatives.description: UCF launched an inaugural mentorship and scholarship program to support undergraduate and graduate students pursuing AI, pairing them with faculty mentors and providing scholarship funding tied to AI coursework and research.
initiatives.significance: Bridges AI for All's literacy mandate with structured research pipelines — a talent-development lever for AI employers recruiting in Central Florida.

investment.funding_source: UCF Institute of Artificial Intelligence and partners
investment.description: Mentorship and scholarship program in AI.

collaborations.description: Internal mentorship tied to IAI industry network.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UCF's Advanced Research Computing Center operates the Stokes CPU cluster (8,800+ Intel Xeon cores, 100 Gb/s HDR100 InfiniBand) and the Newton GPU cluster. Newton combines 42 NVIDIA V100 GPUs (DURIP/UCF), 58 NVIDIA H100 GPUs (EDA), and 32 additional NVIDIA H100 GPUs across 4 nodes with NDR InfiniBand funded specifically by the UCF A.I. Initiative, alongside 660 TB of storage.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UCF's ADA Coordinator Kristeena LaRoue leads digital accessibility training (documents, website, social media, Universal Design for Learning) across institutional tools.

regulatory_context.summary: Florida is actively debating the 'AI Bill of Rights' (SB 482), filed December 22, 2025 under Governor DeSantis. The Senate passed the bill 35-2 on March 4, 2026 and transmitted it to the House on March 5, 2026. If enacted July 1, 2026, it imposes transparency, consumer protection, and educational-entity obligations. A December 2025 Trump executive order attempting to preempt state AI regulation has created tension that Florida leaders have said they will contest. The State University System of Florida Board of Governors has not adopted a system-wide AI policy; individual institutions (including UCF) operate under their own guidelines.

narrative_summary: The University of Central Florida, a 70,000+ student R1 in Orlando and the largest university by enrollment in Florida, has shifted from a distributed collection of AI labs into a coordinated, institution-wide AI enterprise during 2024-2026. Two structures carry the strategy: the Institute of Artificial Intelligence (IAI), launched July 30, 2025 under Trustee Chair Mubarak Shah, which consolidates 25 faculty across the Colleges of Business, Engineering and Computer Science, Medicine and Sciences and absorbs the #8-ranked Center for Research in Computer Vision; and AI for All, led by Kevin Yee as Special Assistant to the Provost for AI, which scales AI fluency across every major through new courses (CAP 1931, PAD 3930), the annual Teaching and Learning with AI Conference, and an Academic Governance Council. UCF's policy posture is comprehensive rather than restrictive: a multi-office Responsible AI Use framework (IT, Libraries, Center for Ethics, CDL, FCTL, Faculty Senate IT Committee, Office of Research) requires disclosure, protects intellectual property and privacy, discourages AI detectors, and leaves syllabus-level rules to faculty. The enterprise tool of choice is Microsoft Copilot with Commercial Data Protection, licensed to every employee and student. On the student-experience side, the Knightbot — a deliberately non-generative, curated-knowledge chatbot powered by Mainstay — serves all 70,000+ students across 50+ units, resolves 85% of queries without human intervention, and cut drop rates by 15% among engaged at-risk students. Research capacity rests on the Newton GPU cluster (including 32 H100s funded directly by the UCF A.I. Initiative) and draws DARPA, IARPA, NSF and USDOE funding. The April 2026 $50M Barry Miller gift — the largest in UCF history — will endow named chairs in AI strategy, fintech, cyber risk, trust and disinformation, and launch a new MS in technology leadership and innovation. Regulatory context is active: Florida's SB 482 AI Bill of Rights (passed Senate March 4, 2026) is expected to impose new educational-entity obligations effective July 1, 2026, and UCF's comprehensive policy posture is well-aligned to absorb it. Gaps remain around formal syllabus-level policy mandates and explicit system-level policy from the Board of Governors.

gaps_acknowledged: No single dated adoption for the Responsible AI Use guidelines could be confirmed; UCF has not published a stand-alone AI Policy.
The AI for All initiative's precise launch date and founding memo were not located in public sources; 2024 is inferred from associated conference cycles.
UCF does not appear to have a named Chief AI Officer role; AI leadership is split between Mubarak Shah (IAI, research) and Kevin Yee (AI for All, teaching).
Dollar amounts for internal UCF AI Initiative funding (especially the Newton H100 tranche) are not publicly disclosed.
Launch dates for the AI, Big Data and Human Impacts certificate and the MS in Data Analytics AI track were not located.
No AI-specific litigation or FERPA controversy specific to UCF was identified in search results.
The State University System of Florida has not published a system-wide AI policy; regulatory exposure flows from Florida SB 482 (pending enactment July 1, 2026).

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: curriculum led.
